---
title: Product Exploration
author: Edward Thompson
output: word_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(ggplot2)
library(GGally)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(rmarkdown)
```

Read in dataset

```{r}
product <- read_csv("train.csv")
```

Replacing missing values

```{r}
product <- product %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric),
                ~replace(., is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE))))
```

Structure and summary

```{r}
str(product)
summary(product)
```

```{r}
product %>%
  group_by(product_code) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = n(),
    FailureRate = mean(failure == "Yes")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(FailureRate))
```

```{r}
ggpairs(product)
```

```{r}
ggpairs(
  product %>%
    select(failure, loading, starts_with("measurement_")),
  aes(color = failure, alpha = 0.2),
  lower = list(
    continuous = wrap("points", size = 0.3)
  ),
  diag = list(
    continuous = wrap("densityDiag")
  )
)
```


```{r}
ggpairs(
  product %>%
    select(
      failure,
      loading,
      measurement_17
    ),
  aes(color = failure, alpha = 0.5),
  lower = list(continuous = wrap("points", size = 0.4))
)
``` 



```{r}
product2 <- product %>%
  mutate(fail_num = ifelse(failure=="Yes",1,0))
correlations <-
product2 %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  cor(use="pairwise.complete.obs")

correlations[,"fail_num"]
sort(correlations[,"fail_num"])
``` 

Loading and Measurment 17 have the two  highest corrolations with the failure variable
```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(failure,
           loading,
           fill=failure)) +
  geom_violin()
``` 
```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(failure,
           measurement_17,
           fill=failure)) +
  geom_violin()
``` 


```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = failure,
           y = loading,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_boxplot()
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = failure,
           y = measurement_17,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_boxplot()
``` 


```{r}
t.test(loading ~ failure,
       data=product)
``` 

```{r}
t.test(measurement_17 ~ failure,
       data=product)
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = loading,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_density(alpha=.4)
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = measurement_1,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_density(alpha=.4)
``` 

```{r}
GGally::ggpairs(
  product,
  columns = 1:8,
  aes(color=failure)
)
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product, aes(x = failure, y = loading, fill = failure)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Loading by Failure Status",
    x = "Failure",
    y = "Loading"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product, aes(x = failure, y = loading, color = failure)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Loading vs. Failure",
    x = "Failure",
    y = "Loading"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
``` 

```{r}
product2 <- product %>%
  mutate(failure_num = ifelse(failure == "Yes", 1, 0))

ggplot(product2, aes(x = loading, y = failure_num)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Loading vs Failure",
    x = "Loading",
    y = "Failure (0 = No, 1 = Yes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = measurement_17,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_density(alpha=.4)
``` 

```{r}
ggplot(product,
       aes(x = measurement_11,
           fill = failure)) +
  geom_density(alpha=.4)
```
